member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridCellRenderer`2
member.summary:
A abstract base class for cell renderer classes that need live views displayed in a cell. You can derive from this class and provide the type of the UIElement you want to show inside cells as type parameter. The class provides strong typed virtual methods for initializing Content of the cell and arranging the cell visuals. See for more details.
The idea behind this class is to provide a place where we can add general code that should be shared for all cell renderer classes in the tree derived from GridVirtualizingCellRendererBase. While this class does at the moment not add meaningful functionality to DataGridCellRenderer we created this extra layer of inheritance to make it easy to share code for the DataGridCellRenderer base class between grid and common assemblies and keep grid control specific code out of the base class. It is currently not possible with C# to the base class as template type parameter.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridCellRenderer`2.AllowRecycle
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether views can be recycled when scrolled out of view. In this case when a cell is scrolled out of view it is moved into a recycle bin and the next time a new element is scrolled into view the element is recovered from the recycle bin and reinitialized with the new Content of the cell.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridCellRenderer`2.SupportsRenderOptimization
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the renderer supports rendering itself directly to the drawing context. When this is possible the UIElement will only be created when the user moves the mouse over the cell or if the UIElement is needed for other reasons, e.g. animate after change. The benefit of rendering directly to the DrawingContext instead of creating the UIElement is a much improved scrolling performance.
member.value: True if the renderer supports optimization, otherwise false. The default value is true.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.VirtualizingCellUIElementBin`1
member.summary:
This class implements a cache of view of the given type parameter T. It is used by the renderer to recycle view elements for cells that were scrolled out of view and delay unloading of views.
This reduces the number of times the views needs to be created or unloaded and instead only the contents of the views will be reinitialized with cell contents.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItem
member.summary: Represents a custom menu item for the SfDataGrid context menu.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItem.CommandProperty
member.summary: Identifies the bindable property.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItem.Command
member.summary: Gets or sets the command to be executed when the menu item is clicked.
member.value: Accepts an implementation. Default is null.
member.remarks: The command will be executed when the menu item is clicked, provided that CanExecute returns true. The command's parameter will be the value of the property. This enables MVVM-style interaction between the UI and business logic.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
## [XAML]
<syncfusion:MenuItem Text="Sort Ascending" Command="{Binding SortAscendingCommand}" CommandParameter="{Binding SelectedColumn}" />
## [C#]
var menuItem = new MenuItem { Text = "Sort Ascending", Command = ContextMenuCommands.SortAscending, CommandParameter = column };

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItem.CommandParameterProperty
member.summary: Identifies the bindable property.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItem.CommandParameter
member.summary: Gets or sets the command parameter to be passed to the command when executed.
member.value: Accepts any . Default is null.
member.remarks: This parameter is passed to the command's Execute and CanExecute methods. For default grid commands, this is typically the DataGridColumn being operated on. It enables context-aware command execution, especially in MVVM scenarios.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
## [XAML]
<syncfusion:MenuItem Text="Sort Ascending" Command="{Binding SortAscendingCommand}" CommandParameter="{Binding SelectedColumn}" />
## [C#]
menuItem.CommandParameter = dataGrid.Columns[0];

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItem.Text
member.summary: Gets or sets the text to be displayed for the menu item.
member.value: It accepts string values, and the default value is empty.
member.remarks: This text is displayed in the menu item's label and should clearly indicate the action that will be performed when the item is clicked.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItem.IsEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the menu item is enabled.
member.value: true if the menu item is enabled; otherwise, false. The default value is true.
member.remarks: Disabled menu items appear grayed out and cannot be clicked. This property can be used to conditionally enable or disable operations based on context, such as clipboard availability or user permissions.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
## [XAML]
<syncfusion:MenuItem Text="Paste" IsEnabled="{Binding IsPasteAvailable}" />
## [C#]
pasteMenuItem.IsEnabled = Clipboard.HasText;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItem.Icon
member.summary: Gets or sets the icon to be displayed before the menu item text.
member.value: Accepts a such as an or . Default is null.
member.remarks: Icons can help users quickly identify menu item functions. This can be an view for bitmap icons or a view for vector icons. If no icon is provided, only the text will be displayed.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItemCollection
member.summary: Represents a collection of context menu items used in .
member.remarks: This class supports declarative definition of context menu items, typically used in grid-level menus such as header, record, or summary. It is commonly defined in XAML using the context menu properties.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to use the in the property.
## [XAML]
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid x:Name="dataGrid" ItemsSource="{Binding YourData}" AutoGenerateColumns="True"> <syncfusion:SfDataGrid.HeaderContextMenu> <syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text="Sort Ascending" Command="{Binding SortAscendingCommand}" /> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text="Sort Descending" Command="{Binding SortDescendingCommand}" /> </syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid.HeaderContextMenu> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#]
dataGrid.HeaderContextMenu = new MenuItemCollection { new MenuItem { Text = "Sort Ascending", Command = ViewModel.SortAscendingCommand }, new MenuItem { Text = "Sort Descending", Command = ViewModel.SortDescendingCommand } };

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItemCollection.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.MenuItemCollection.Items
member.summary: Gets the collection of menu items.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid
member.summary: A data bound grid control that displays the data from data source in a tabular view. The control allows you to display different types of columns, sort and select the rows.
member.remarks: To bind the to data, set the property to an implementation. Each row in the data grid is bound to an object in the data source, and each column in the data grid is bound to a property of the data object. In order for the user interface to update automatically when items are added to or removed from the source data, the must be bound to a collection that implements the interface, such as an . To automatically reflect property changes, the objects in the source collection must implement the interface.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.NotifyEventsToParentDataGrid
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether to notify details view DataGrid or child data DataGrid events to the parent DataGrid. This allows event handling to be centralized in the parent DataGrid, eliminating the need to handle events at each hierarchical level.
member.value: true if the child DataGrid notifies events to the parent DataGrid; otherwise, false. The default value is false.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
## [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid.DetailsViewDefinition> <syncfusion:GridViewDefinition RelationalColumn="OrderDetails"> <syncfusion:GridViewDefinition.DataGrid> <syncfusion:SfDataGrid x:Name="FirstDetailsViewGrid" NotifyEventsToParentDataGrid="True"> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid> </syncfusion:GridViewDefinition.DataGrid> </syncfusion:GridViewDefinition> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid.DetailsViewDefinition>
## [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
FirstDetailsViewGrid.NotifyEventsToParentDataGrid = true;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.AllowRowHoverHighlighting

member.value: true to enable row hover background effect; otherwise, false.

members.member:
The number of non-scrolling columns at the left side of
The default value is 0.
value should be less than the number of columns displayed in the view. For example, if you have 5 columns in the view, you can set the `FrozenColumnCount` value to a maximum of 4.
property.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.FooterFrozenColumnCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of non-scrolling columns at the right side of the . By default, no columns are frozen at the footer.
member.value: 0 by default. Set a value less than the number of visible columns to freeze columns at the right side.
member.remarks: The must be less than the total number of columns displayed in the view.

example.code: dataGrid.FooterFrozenColumnCount = 1;

members.member:
The number of non-scrolling rows at the top of
The default value is 0.
value should be less than the number of rows displayed in the view. For example, if you have 10 rows in the view, you can set the `FrozenRowCount` value to a maximum of 9.
property.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.FooterFrozenRowCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of non-scrolling rows at the bottom of the . By default, no rows are frozen at the footer.
member.value: 0 by default. Set a value less than the number of visible rows to freeze rows at the bottom.
member.remarks: The must be less than the total number of rows displayed in the view.

example.code: dataGrid.FooterFrozenRowCount = 1;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.ItemsSource
member.summary: Gets or sets an external data source for , to display the data in the grid. It supports the data sources such as , , and so on. property helps to bind the with the collection of objects.
member.value: The items source of the .
member.remarks: To bind the to data, set the property to an implementation. Each row in the datagrid is bound to an object in the data source, and each column in the data grid is bound to a property of the data object. In order to update the user interface automatically when the items are added to or removed from the source data, the must be bound to a collection that implements the interface, such as an . To automatically reflect property changes, the objects in the source collection must implement the interface.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.CellRenderers
member.summary: Gets the that contains the cell renderers in . The manages the creation of cells view objects when the cell is scrolled into view and also unloading of the views. Contains logics to place the UIElement inside cells in display and edit mode.
member.value: The collection containing the renderers in the .
member.remarks:
The manages the creation of cells view objects when the cell is scrolled into view and also unloading of the views. Contains logics to place the UIElement inside cells in display and edit mode. The user can implement the renderer by extending it from the renderer he needs and customize the type of cell to be loaded for each column type in editing and normal mode.
Note:When the user overrides the renderer, he needs to replace it in the collection. For example, if the user implements the class, to customize the cell in the , then he needs to replace it in the collection.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.AllowDiagonalScrolling
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether diagonal scrolling is allowed in the .
member.value: Accepts values. The default value is true.
member.remarks: When set to false, users can only scroll either horizontally or vertically at a time, preventing diagonal scrolling.
member.example:
The following example demonstrates how to set the property in .
# [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<dataGrid:SfDataGrid AllowDiagonalScrolling="False" />
# [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
sfDataGrid.AllowDiagonalScrolling = false;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.AllowAsyncScrolling
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether asynchronous scrolling is enabled in the . This is a bindable property.
member.value: Accepts values. The default value is false.
member.remarks: Enables asynchronous rendering during large scroll jumps to improve performance during rapid offset changes. This is applicable for all types of columns on Windows platform.
member.example:
The following example demonstrates how to set the property in .
# [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<dataGrid:SfDataGrid AllowAsyncScrolling="True" />
# [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
sfDataGrid.AllowAsyncScrolling = true;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.ShowRowHeader
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include the row header or not. By default, nothing will be displayed in the row header. However, when property is set, the datagrid loads the given template as the content of the row header cells. This is a bindable property.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.RowHeaderWidth

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.EmptyView
member.summary: Gets or sets a content or view that will be displayed when there are no records in
member.value: The Empty view’s type is object, so it accepts both string and view type values
member.remarks: It helps to identify the items source is empty instead of showing a blank view.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property. # [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<local:SfDataGrid EmptyView = "No records">
</local:SfDataGrid>
or
# [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<local:SfDataGrid> <loacl:SfDataGrid.EmptyView> <Label Text = "No records found" HorizontalTextAlignment="Center"/> </local:SfDataGrid.EmptyView> </local:SfDataGrid>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
dataGrid.EmptyView = "no records";

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.EmptyViewTemplate
member.summary: Gets or sets a data template that will be displayed for empty view in
member.value: It accepts the data template and template selector value.
member.remarks: It helps to identify the items source is empty instead of showing a blank view.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.VerticalOverScrollMode
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating what effect should be applied when is vertically over-scrolled.
member.value: The of . The default value is for Android and for Ios.
member.remarks: The The property has no effect on desktop platforms, as they do not support bounce effects.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
## [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid VerticalOverScrollMode="Bounce"> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
var dataGrid = new SfDataGrid(); dataGrid.VerticalOverScrollMode = VerticalOverScrollMode.Bounce;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.AutoGenerateRelations
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the relations for Master-Details View are generated automatically.
member.value: true if relations are auto-generated; otherwise, false. The default value is false.
member.remarks: You can cancel or customize the relation being created in the event handler.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
## [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid AutoGenerateRelations="True"> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
var dataGrid = new SfDataGrid(); dataGrid.AutoGenerateRelations = true;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.SelectedDetailsViewDataGrid
member.summary: Gets or sets the DetailsViewDataGrid that is currently selected.
member.value: Returns the selected details view DataGrid.
member.remarks: You can access the properties and their values of the selected details view DataGrid through this property.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.DetailsViewPadding
member.summary: Gets or sets the padding of the DetailsViewDataGrid.
member.value: The padding for the DetailsViewDataGrid.
member.remarks: The needs to be defined on its parent DataGrid when the child grid requires padding.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
## [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid DetailsViewPadding="10, 10, 10, 10"> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
var dataGrid = new SfDataGrid(); dataGrid.DetailsViewPadding = new Thickness(10, 10, 10, 10);

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.DetailsViewDefaultStyle
member.summary: Gets or sets the style applied to all the DetailsViewDataGrid.
member.value: The style that is applied to all the DetailsViewDataGrid. The default value is null.
member.remarks: To define a for a DetailsViewDataGrid, specify a TargetType of .
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
## [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid> <syncfusion:SfDataGrid.DetailsViewDataGridStyle> <syncfusion:DataGridStyle BackgroundColor="LightGray" /> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid.DetailsViewDataGridStyle> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
var dataGrid = new SfDataGrid(); dataGrid.DetailsViewDataGridStyle = new DataGridStyle { BackgroundColor = Colors.LightGray };

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.CopyOption
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates how the content is copied from control to the clipboard.This is bindable property.
member.value: One of the enumeration that specifies how the content is copied from control to the clipboard.
member.remarks: You can cancel or customize the copy operation through and event handlers in SfDataGrid.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
## [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid CopyOption="CopyData"> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
var dataGrid = new SfDataGrid(); dataGrid.CopyOption = DataGridCopyOption.CopyData;

members.member:
Gets or sets a value that indicates how the clipboard value is pasted.This is bindable property.
One of the enumeration that specifies how the clipboard value is pasted.
You can customize or cancel the paste operations through and event handlers in SfDataGrid.
/// Here is an example of how to set the property.
## [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid PasteOption="PasteData"> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
var dataGrid = new SfDataGrid(); dataGrid.PasteOption = DataGridPasteOption.PasteData;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.CopyPasteController
member.summary: Gets or sets an instance of the class which controls the copy and paste operations in SfDataGrid.This is bindable property.
member.value: An instance of class that derives from class. The default value is null.
member.remarks: The class provides various properties and virtual methods to customize its operations.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to access the property.
## [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid x:Name="dataGrid" />
## [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
dataGrid.ClipboardController.Copy();

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.AllowGrouping
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the UI grouping is allowed in the DataGrid. When set to true, users can drag column headers into the GroupDropArea to group rows based on the column values.
member.value: true if grouping is enabled; otherwise, false. The default value is false.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.GroupDropAreaText
member.summary: Gets or sets the text displayed in the GroupDropArea when no columns are grouped. This text provides guidance to users about dragging column headers for grouping.
member.value: A representing the instructional text shown in the GroupDropArea. The default value is "Drag and drop a column header to grouping".

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.GroupDropAreaHeight
member.summary: Gets or sets the height of the GroupDropArea displayed at the top of the DataGrid. This area is used for grouping columns by dragging their headers.
member.value: A value that specifies the height of the GroupDropArea in device-independent units. The default value is 40.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.HeaderContextMenu
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of menu items to be displayed in the header context menu.
member.value: A collection of menu items for the header context menu.
member.remarks: This property allows customization of the context menu that appears when interacting with header cells.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to define the property.
## [XAML]
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid x:Name=""dataGrid"" ItemsSource=""{Binding YourData}"" AutoGenerateColumns=""True""> <syncfusion:SfDataGrid.HeaderContextMenu> <syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Sort Ascending"" Command=""{Binding SortAscendingCommand}"" /> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Sort Descending"" Command=""{Binding SortDescendingCommand}"" /> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Group by Column"" Command=""{Binding GroupByColumnCommand}"" /> </syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid.HeaderContextMenu> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#]
dataGrid.HeaderContextMenu = new MenuItemCollection { new MenuItem { Text = ""Sort Ascending"", Command = ViewModel.SortAscendingCommand }, new MenuItem { Text = ""Sort Descending"", Command = ViewModel.SortDescendingCommand }, new MenuItem { Text = ""Group by Column"", Command = ViewModel.GroupByColumnCommand } };

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.RecordContextMenu
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of menu items to be displayed in the record context menu.
member.value: A collection of menu items for the record context menu.
member.remarks: The record context menu appears when right-clicking (on desktop platforms) or long-pressing (on mobile platforms) on a data row.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to define the property.
## [XAML]
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid x:Name=""dataGrid"" ItemsSource=""{Binding YourData}"" AutoGenerateColumns=""True""> <syncfusion:SfDataGrid.RecordContextMenu> <syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Edit"" Command=""{Binding EditRecordCommand}"" /> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Delete"" Command=""{Binding DeleteRecordCommand}"" /> </syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid.RecordContextMenu> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#]
dataGrid.RecordContextMenu = new MenuItemCollection { new MenuItem { Text = ""Edit"", Command = ViewModel.EditRecordCommand }, new MenuItem { Text = ""Delete"", Command = ViewModel.DeleteRecordCommand } };

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.GroupCaptionContextMenu
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of menu items to be displayed in the group caption context menu.
member.value: A collection of menu items for the group caption context menu.
member.remarks: The group caption context menu appears when right-clicking (on desktop platforms) or long-pressing (on mobile platforms) on a group caption cell.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to define the property.
## [XAML]
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid x:Name=""dataGrid"" ItemsSource=""{Binding YourGroupedData}"" AutoGenerateColumns=""True"" ShowGroupDropArea=""True"" AllowGrouping=""True""> <syncfusion:SfDataGrid.GroupCaptionContextMenu> <syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Expand"" Command=""{Binding ExpandGroupCommand}"" /> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Collapse"" Command=""{Binding CollapseGroupCommand}"" /> </syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid.GroupCaptionContextMenu> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#]
dataGrid.GroupCaptionContextMenu = new MenuItemCollection { new MenuItem { Text = ""Expand"", Command = ViewModel.ExpandGroupCommand }, new MenuItem { Text = ""Collapse"", Command = ViewModel.CollapseGroupCommand } };

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.GroupSummaryContextMenu
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of menu items to be displayed in the group summary context menu.
member.value: A collection of menu items for the group summary context menu.
member.remarks: The group summary context menu appears when right-clicking (on desktop platforms) or long-pressing (on mobile platforms) on a group summary row.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to define the property.
## [XAML]
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid x:Name=""dataGrid"" ItemsSource=""{Binding YourGroupedData}"" AutoGenerateColumns=""True"" ShowGroupDropArea=""True"" AllowGrouping=""True""> <syncfusion:SfDataGrid.GroupSummaryContextMenu> <syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Clear Summary"" Command=""{Binding ClearSummaryCommand}"" /> </syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid.GroupSummaryContextMenu> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#]
dataGrid.GroupSummaryContextMenu = new MenuItemCollection { new MenuItem { Text = ""Clear Summary"", Command = ViewModel.ClearSummaryCommand } };

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfDataGrid.TableSummaryContextMenu
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of menu items to be displayed in the table summary context menu.
member.value: A collection of menu items for the table summary context menu.
member.remarks: The table summary context menu appears when right-clicking (on desktop platforms) or long-pressing (on mobile platforms) on a table summary row.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to define the property.
## [XAML]
<syncfusion:SfDataGrid x:Name=""dataGrid"" ItemsSource=""{Binding YourData}"" AutoGenerateColumns=""True"" ShowTableSummary=""True""> <syncfusion:SfDataGrid.TableSummaryContextMenu> <syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Count"" Command=""{Binding CountCommand}"" /> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Max"" Command=""{Binding MaxCommand}"" /> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Min"" Command=""{Binding MinCommand}"" /> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Average"" Command=""{Binding AverageCommand}"" /> <syncfusion:MenuItem Text=""Sum"" Command=""{Binding SumCommand}"" /> </syncfusion:MenuItemCollection> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid.TableSummaryContextMenu> </syncfusion:SfDataGrid>
## [C#]
dataGrid.TableSummaryContextMenu = new MenuItemCollection { new MenuItem { Text = ""Count"", Command = ViewModel.CountCommand }, new MenuItem { Text = ""Max"", Command = ViewModel.MaxCommand }, new MenuItem { Text = ""Min"", Command = ViewModel.MinCommand }, new MenuItem { Text = ""Average"", Command = ViewModel.AverageCommand }, new MenuItem { Text = ""Sum"", Command = ViewModel.SumCommand } };

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.CoveredCellInfo.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Returns a boolean value indicating if the passed object is Equal to the current object. Equality is defined as object equality for reference types and bitwise equality for value types using a loader trick to replace Equals with EqualsValue for value types.

member.returns: Returns a boolean value indicating whether the passed object is equal to the current object.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.CoveredCellInfo.GetHashCode
member.summary:
GetHashCode is intended to serve as a hash function for this object. Based on the contents of the object, the hash function will return a suitable value with a relatively random distribution over the various inputs.
The default implementation returns the [....] block index for this instance. Calling it on the same object multiple times will return the same value, so it will technically meet the needs of a hash function, but it's less than ideal. Objects (especially value classes) should override this method.
member.returns: Return a suitable value with a relatively random distribution over the various inputs based on the contents of the object.

member.summary: Specifies the copy operation is disabled.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridCopyOption.CopyData
member.summary: Specifies the values of selected cells/rows is copied from SfDataGrid to the clipboard.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridCopyOption.CutData
member.summary: Specifies the values of selected cells/rows is cut from SfDataGrid to the clipboard.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridCopyOption.IncludeFormat
member.summary: Specifies the values of selected cells/rows along with its format is copy/cut from SfDataGrid to the clipboard.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridCopyOption.IncludeHeaders
member.summary: Specifies the values of selected cells/rows along with its column header values is copy/cut from SfDataGrid to the clipboard.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridCopyOption.IncludeHiddenColumn
member.summary: Specifies the values of selected rows along with hidden column values is copy/cut from SfDataGrid to the clipboard.
member.remarks: You can't able to copy the hidden column values when SelectionUnit as Cell.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridPasteOption
member.summary: Defines the constants that specify the possible paste option in SfDataGrid.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridPasteOption.None
member.summary: Specifies the paste support is disabled.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridPasteOption.PasteData
member.summary: Specifies the clipboard copied content is pasted to SfDataGrid.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridPasteOption.ExcludeFirstLine
member.summary: Specifies the clipboard copied content is pasted to SfDataGrid except the first line.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridPasteOption.IncludeHiddenColumn
member.summary: Specifies the clipboard copied content with hidden column values is pasted to SfDataGrid.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridAddNewRowPosition
member.summary: Defines the constants that specify whether the AddNewRow positioned at top or bottom of body region in SfDataGrid.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridAddNewRowPosition.None
member.summary: No AddNewRow will be placed.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridAddNewRowPosition.Top
member.summary: Specifies that AddNewRow will be placed at top of the body region.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridAddNewRowPosition.FixedTop
member.summary: Specifies that AddNewRow will be placed at top of the body region in frozen state.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridAddNewRowPosition.Bottom
member.summary: Specifies that AddNewRow will be placed at bottom of the body region.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridScrollingMode
member.summary: Describes the possible scrolling modes that a control has.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridScrollingMode.Pixel
member.summary: Lets the grid to scroll based on pixel values.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridScrollingMode.Line
member.summary: Lets the grid to scroll based on line values.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridScrollingMode.PixelLine
member.summary: Lets the grid to scroll based on pixel values and auto scrolls to the start or end or a row or column based on the clipped value.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfMultiColumnComboBox.PopupHeight
member.summary: Gets or sets the height of pop-up.
member.value: The height of the pop-up. The is set as the default height of the pop-up.
member.remarks: When is true, the given pop-up height won't considered. Pop-up height will be calculated based on number of rows in a pop-up. When is false, then the given Pop-up height will be considered. if the given pop-up height is greater than the available height, then the pop-up height will set based on the available maximum height by considering top and bottom side of the window. if the given pop-up height is less than the PopupMinHeight, then will set as Pop-up height by not considering .

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfMultiColumnComboBox.PopupWidthProperty

member.remarks: The identifier for the bindable property.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.SfMultiColumnComboBox.PopupWidth
member.summary: Gets or sets the width of the pop-up.
member.value: The width of the pop-up. The is set as the default width of the pop-up.
member.remarks: When is true, the given Pop-up Width won't considered. Pop-up width will be calculated based on the number of columns in a SfDataGrid. When is false, then the given Pop-up width will be considered. if the given pop-up width is greater than the available width, then the pop-up width will be set based on the available maximum width by considering left and right side of the window. if the given pop-up width is less than the PopupMinWidth, then will set as Pop-up Width by not considering .

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridSummaryColumn
member.summary: Represents a class that defines the summary calculation based on a column.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridSummaryColumn.Template
member.summary: Gets or sets the template that is used to display the contents of a .

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridSummaryColumn.CustomAggregate
member.summary: Gets or sets the custom aggregate of the .
member.remarks: DataGrid provides the built in aggregate types for calculating the values for the summary column in the SummaryType of the.The user can implement their own aggregate function when the built in aggregate function does not meet his requirement using this property.When the user defines his own aggregate function in the CustomAggregate, set the as Custom.

example.code: <ContentPage.Resources> <local:CustomAggregate x:Key="customAggregate"></local:CustomAggreate> </ContentPage.Resources> <sfGrid:SfDataGrid.TableSummaryRows> <sfGrid:DataGridTableSummaryRow Title="Standard Deviation:{TableSummary}" ShowSummaryInRow = "True"> <sfGrid:DataGridTableSummaryRow.SummaryColumns> <sfGrid:DataGridSummaryColumn Name="TableSummary" CustomAggregate="{StaticResource customAggregate}" Format="{{StdDev}" MappingName="OrderID" SummaryType="Custom"/> </sfGrid:DataGridTableSummaryRow.SummaryColumns> </sfGrid:DataGridTableSummaryRow>
<syncfusion:DataGridSummaryColumn Name="TotalSalary" Format="{}{Sum:C0}" MappingName="Salary" SummaryType="Custom" />

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridSummaryColumn.Format
member.summary:
Gets or sets the format to be applied to the summary value.
The user can format the summary value by setting the appropriate format after the aggregate function followed by a colon(:) in this property.

member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the summary column that helps to denote the in

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridSummaryColumn.SummaryType
member.summary: Gets or sets the type of the summary column for calculating values.
member.remarks: DataGrid provides the built in aggregate types for calculating the values for the summary column in the SummaryType.User can implement their own aggregate function when the built in aggregate function does not meet his requirement using CustomAggregate property.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridColumn.ValueBinding
member.summary: Gets or sets the value binding that associates the column with a property in the data source. The value of the property in the underlying model object bound to the value binding will be the Content of the grid cell in edit mode.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridColumn.LoadUIView
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to load the UI element into the or draw the cell value on Android, iOS and MAC.
member.value: True if the UI element is loaded in the grid cell; otherwise, false. The default value is true.
member.remarks: By default, the loads the UI element in the grid cell. Set property to false to draw the cell values in the grid cell. This is applicable only for the Android, iOS and MAC platform. It always loads the UI element for the remaining platforms.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridColumn.LineBreakMode
member.summary: Gets or sets LineBreakMode for the . This is a bindable property.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridColumn.HeaderLineBreakMode
member.summary: Gets or sets the LineBreak modes for the column header.
member.value: It accepts enum values.
member.remarks: This property allows you to breaking the text using line break mode options.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property
# [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<local:SfDataGrid> <local:SfDataGrid.Columns> <local:DataGridTextColumn HeaderLineBreakMode="WordWrap"/> </local:SfDataGrid.Columns> </local:SfDataGrid>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
datagrid.Columns.Add(new DataGridTextColumn() { HeaderLineBreakMode = LineBreakMode.WordWrap, });

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridColumn.CultureInfo
member.summary: Gets or sets the culture used for formatting the Content of record cells in the column. enables the user to apply different culture to the records in the cells by setting this property.
member.value: The Culture used for formatting the Content of record cells in the column.
member.remarks: allows you to apply different for the DataGridColumns by using this property. Assign the FormatString to this property based on the type of the property the column is associated with to format the value. The user can use different StringFormats to customize the value displayed in the record cells.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridColumn.UseBindingValue
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the binding gets property value from column wrapper instead of reflecting value from the item properties.
member.remarks: This property helps to perform , filtering, grouping operations on complex or indexer properties.
member.value: true if the property value gets from column wrapper; otherwise, false. The default value is false.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridColumn.CellTemplate
member.summary: Gets or sets the used to display the content of the cells.
member.value: A that defines the visual structure of the cell content.
member.remarks: Setting the CellTemplate affects only the content of the cells in the column.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridColumn.SetCellBoundValue
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the BindingContextHelper should be used for binding in cell templates.
member.value: true if the BindingContextHelper is used to provide both Record (row data) and Value (cell value) to the template binding context; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: When this property is set to true, the cell template's binding context will include both the entire data record and the specific cell value. This enables more flexible and powerful data bindings within the cell template, especially when multiple fields or contextual logic are required.
member.example:
The following example demonstrates how to use the SetCellBoundValue property in XAML:
<local:SfDataGrid ItemsSource="{Binding Orders}"> <local:SfDataGrid.Columns> <local:DataGridNumericColumn MappingName="OrderID" SetCellBoundValue="True" CellTemplate="{StaticResource cellTemplate}" /> <local:DataGridTextColumn MappingName="CustomerName" SetCellBoundValue="True" CellTemplate="{StaticResource cellTemplate}" /> </local:SfDataGrid.Columns> </local:SfDataGrid>

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridTextColumn
member.summary: Represents a column that hosts textual Content in its cells.

remarks.para: Use DataGridTextColumn to display columns of data as text. To create a column, add it to the collection and to populate the data to the column set the to the property in the data source. dataGrid.Columns.Add(new DataGridTextColumn() { MappingName = "OrderID" });.

The data can also be populated to the column by using the DataGridColumn.DisplayBinding property. The Binding property is applied to the view created in the column. The for the view in each cell is the data item for the row the cell is present. Therefore, to set up the binding you only have to set the . Optionally, you can specify a if you want to convert the data.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridRowInfo.GetHashCode
member.summary:
GetHashCode is intended to serve as a hash function for this object. Based on the contents of the object, the hash function will return a suitable value with a relatively random distribution over the various inputs.
The default implementation returns the [....] block index for this instance. Calling it on the same object multiple times will return the same value, so it will technically meet the needs of a hash function, but it's less than ideal. Objects (especially value classes) should override this method.
member.returns: Return a suitable value with a relatively random distribution over the various inputs based on the contents of the object.

member.summary: Returns a boolean indicating if the passed object is Equal to this. Equality is defined as object equality for reference types and bitwise equality for value types using a loader trick to replace Equals with EqualsValue for value types.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridStyle.HeaderGridLineColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the color of the grid lines in the column header and stacked header cells.
member.value: It accepts values, the default color is Color.FromRgba("#CAC4D0").
member.remarks: This property allows you to set the color of the grid lines in the column header and stacked header cells for better visual distinction.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
# [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<local:SfDataGrid> <local:SfDataGrid.DefaultStyle> <local:DataGridStyle HeaderGridLineColor="Gray" /> </local:SfDataGrid.DefaultStyle> </local:SfDataGrid>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
var dataGrid = new SfDataGrid(); DataGridStyle style = new DataGridStyle() { HeaderGridLineColor = Colors.Gray }; dataGrid.DefaultStyle = style;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridStyle.GridLineStrokeThickness
member.summary: Gets or sets the stroke thickness of the grid lines.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridStyle.HeaderGridLineStrokeThickness
member.summary: Gets or sets the stroke thickness of the grid line in the column header and stacked header cells.
member.value: It accepts values, and the default thickness is 1.0.
member.remarks: This property allows you to customize the appearance of the header grid line by adjusting its stroke thickness in the column header and stacked header cells.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
[XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<local:SfDataGrid> <local:SfDataGrid.DefaultStyle> <local:DataGridStyle HeaderGridLineStrokeThickness="2.0" /> </local:SfDataGrid.DefaultStyle> </local:SfDataGrid>
[C#](#tab/tabid-2)
var dataGrid = new SfDataGrid(); DataGridStyle style = new DataGridStyle() { HeaderGridLineStrokeThickness = 2.0f }; dataGrid.DefaultStyle = style;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridStyle.FreezePaneLineStrokeThickness
member.summary: Gets or sets the stroke thickness of the freeze pane line in the data grid.
member.value: It accepts values, and the default thickness is 1.
member.remarks: This property allows you to customize the appearance of the freeze pane line by adjusting its stroke thickness. The freeze pane line visually separates frozen rows or columns from the scrollable area in the grid.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
[XAML///
<local:SfDataGrid> <local:SfDataGrid.DefaultStyle> <local:DataGridStyle FreezePaneLineStrokeThickness="2" /> </local:SfDataGrid.DefaultStyle> </local:SfDataGrid>
[C#](#tab/tabid-1)
var dataGrid = new SfDataGrid(); DataGridStyle style = new DataGridStyle() { FreezePaneLineStrokeThickness = 2 }; dataGrid.DefaultStyle = style;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.DataGridStyle.RowHoveredBackground
member.summary: Gets or sets the background color for data rows when the mouse pointer hovers over them.
member.value: The background color for the hovered row. The default value is a brush with color #141C1B1F.
member.remarks: This property provides visual feedback when users hover over rows, improving user experience on desktop platforms. The hover effect is primarily supported on Windows and macOS platforms.
member.example:
Here is an example of how to set the property.
# [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<local:SfDataGrid> <local:SfDataGrid.DefaultStyle> <local:DataGridStyle RowHoveredBackground="#20000000" /> </local:SfDataGrid.DefaultStyle> </local:SfDataGrid>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
var dataGrid = new SfDataGrid(); DataGridStyle style = new DataGridStyle() { RowHoveredBackground = new SolidColorBrush(Color.FromArgb("#20000000")) }; dataGrid.DefaultStyle = style;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.DataGrid.ValidationHelper.IsFocusSetBack
member.summary: Gets or sets the value that indicates to prevent the and events to be raised more than once when click on FilterToggleButton.
member.remarks: If IsFocusSetBack true then end user can't move out of the current cell by tapping on DataGridCell. While doing key navigation and clicking on FilterToggleButton IsFocusSetBack needs to be in false to do Validation.
